channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: How President Trump is expanding the powers of the presidency
item.description: President Trump is pushing the limits of presidential power, and the courts are pushing back. Coming up at 9 a.m. on Monday, MPR News host Angela Davis talks about what a president can and cannot do under the law.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Maja Beckstrom, MPR News

item.title: Hungary's transformation into an 'electoral autocracy' has parallels to Trump's second term
item.description: U.S. conservatives have looked to Hungary's Viktor Orbán as an inspiration for a right-wing America, and the prime minister has become a close ally of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike And Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Legislative immunity is a privilege in most states. A speeding ticket could change that in Arizona
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — When a police officer gave state Sen. Mark Finchem a speeding ticket, the Arizona Republican didn't need to worry about the consequences.
item.dc:creator: GABRIEL SANDOVAL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's mugshot spotted hanging in ornate frame near Oval Office
item.description: Social media posts showed a framed front page 2023 mugshot of President Trump from the New York Post near the Oval Office in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fearful immigrants ask Florida activist to sign guardianship papers for their children
item.description: Since President Donald Trump took office, immigrants in the U.S. illegally have changed their travel patterns and try to stay home, out of fear they could be deported.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge extends order blocking Musk’s DOGE team from Treasury payment system
item.description: A federal judge in New York on Friday extended a temporary order blocking the Elon Musk-run Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Treasury Department payment systems.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump rolls back CFPB, USAID and Department of Education: Where things stand
item.description: President Trump supercharged his overhaul of government agencies this week that included a fresh round of a widespread firings.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Foreign aid freeze temporarily lifted, but damage may already be done
item.description: Some Republican lawmakers introduced legislation to find a way to work around Trump administration cuts to USAID.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: The art of the deal? Zelenskyy says a Ukraine-Russia agreement must come through Trump negotiations
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy appealed to President Donald Trump's history as a dealmaker on Friday, offering flattering words as he sought the U.S. president's leadership in negotiations that could end the Russia-Ukraine conflict.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS, MONIKA SCISLOWSKA and STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: McConnell tests the strengths and limits of his power opposing a trio of Trump's Cabinet nominees
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — No longer in charge, Sen. Mitch McConnell has been speaking his mind, the long-serving GOP leader rejecting President Donald Trump’s more high-profile Cabinet nominees — alone at times, among the Republicans, casting his no votes.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Order to drop New York Mayor Adams' case roils Justice Department as high-ranking officials resign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Manhattan’s top federal prosecutor, Danielle Sassoon, and five high-ranking Justice Department officials resigned Thursday after she refused an order to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams — a stunning escalation in a dayslong standoff over the Trump admini
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Suspended Hillsborough Democrat defends online posts some say are antisemitic
item.description: The Hillsborough County Democratic Executive Committee has suspended a member until January 2027 over posts online and wording in email messages.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada joins lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power
item.description: Democratic Nevada Attorney General Aaron Ford joined more than a dozen other attorneys general Thursday in filing a lawsuit challenging Elon Musk's executive power.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CA, 18 other states' AGs celebrate injunction against Trump's citizenship order
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA)—California Attorney General Rob Bonta and 18 other state attorneys general have issued a statement after another judge blocked President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to terminate birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: At McMahon’s confirmation hearing, it was the Education Department on trial
item.description: In her confirmation hearing, Linda McMahon handled tough questions from Democrats, and affirmed President Trump’s plans to shrink, and potentially eliminate, the department.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner and Jonaki Mehta, MPR News

item.title: Judge blocks Trump’s gender-affirming care executive orders nationwide
item.description: BALTIMORE — A federal judge Thursday blocked parts of two executive orders issued by President Trump that collectively seek to restrict gender-affirming care. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Measure for county to back ban on trans women in sports fails
item.description: An effort to get San Diego County to throw its support behind a bill in Congress that would ban transgender girls and women from competing in women's sports failed when it was brought forward at the Board of Supervisors on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Sen. Ted Cruz’s list of ’woke’ science includes self-driving cars and solar eclipses
item.description: Sen. Ted Cruz, R-Texas, says the National Science Foundation has given money to thousands of “woke DEI” studies. Researchers say that’s misrepresenting science and disparaging important research.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert, MPR News

item.title: Could Kamala Harris be the next California governor? Here's how she's polling
item.description: Kamala Harris holds a substantial lead over other potential candidates to succeed California Gov. Gavin Newsom in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr. confirmed as Health and Human Services secretary
item.description: Kennedy confirmed in party-line vote, with final tally of 58-42. McConnell was only Republican lawmaker to vote no.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota lawmaker wants to ban new billboard signs
item.description: Sen. John Marty, DFL-Roseville, said the signs detract from the state's natural scenic beauty and pose a safety concern for motorists. Billboard companies say the bill would hurt local businesses and curtail First Amendment rights.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Mobile pharmacy brings meds to Winthrop customers after losing brick and mortar store
item.description: Some Minnesota pharmacies are closing their doors because of lower reimbursement rates for prescription drugs and dwindling profit margins — especially for pharmacies with a large customer base on public insurance.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Legislature OKs bill targeting high egg prices, allowing suspension of cage-free requirement
item.description: Nevada lawmakers on Wednesday gave final approval to a bill that could bring down the price of eggs, clearing the way for Gov. Joe Lombardo to give it his signature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump upends U.S. policy on Ukraine and says he and Putin have agreed to begin talks on ending the war
item.description: President Donald Trump upended three years of U.S. policy toward Ukraine on Wednesday, saying that he and Russian President Vladimir Putin had agreed to begin negotiations on ending the war following a dramatic prisoner swap.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Hochul reacts to pending dismissal of corruption case against Mayor Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- The pending dismissal of the corruption case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams has partners in running the city and political opponents raising concerns about whether Adams can run the city. Gov. Kathy Hochul even said she does not know if the mayor is “compromised.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Musk defends DOGE alongside Trump in striking Oval Office moment
item.description: Elon Musk on Tuesday defended the work of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) alongside President Trump, who invited Musk to speak and sat at the resolute desk as the tech mogul gave a lengthy address.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Pope criticizes Trump administration's immigration policies
item.description: Pope Francis criticized President Donald Trump’s mass deportation plans in a letter urging compassion and the preservation of dignity for migrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Florida lawmakers to begin third special session on illegal immigration
item.description: After a particularly contentious few weeks, Florida lawmakers will be heading back to Tallahassee Tuesday for the purpose of passing legislation on illegal immigration — this time with the governor's support.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Battleground Florida: Officials discuss tariffs, LGBTQ policies
item.description: University of Tampa Economics Professor Michael Coon explained the purpose of tariffs and the impact they will likely have on businesses and families in Tampa Bay.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats launch portal for whistleblowers in Trump administration
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) and Sen. Gary Peters (D-Mich.), the ranking member of the Senate Homeland Security Committee, said they are launching a new portal for whistleblowers to expose wrongdoing and abuses of power in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5
